title: Friday Sermon 7 July 1995
description:
All of the Divine attributes are deeply related to the Divine attributes mentioned in Sura al-Fatiha.  The attribute al-Haq [The Truth] is deeply connected to the attributes mentioned in Sura al-Fatiha.  One meaning of Haq is justice and fairness.  Also the attribute of al-Haq has a connection with al-Rahman [The Gracious].  Usually when one treats a person with immense grace one deviates from the path of fairness but those who adopt the hues of the attributes of Almighty God they fall under the attribute of al-Haq as well as manifesting Grace.

An example can be given in an incident of Hazrat Mir Hamid Shah.  The Gracious God has also created justice so if you are to form a relationship with the Gracious God then lay out the paths of truth.  One cannot connect with the Divine attributes by chanting them on one's tongue and true devotion can only be offered when the Divine attributes circumambulate in one's heart.  

It is important to stand truthful in order to form a relationship with Almighty God.  Then if one succeeds through the tribulations with recourse to these attributes one would at last form a relationship with Him who is the complete Truth.  Then He would show such Signs in your support for which there can be no comparison in this world.

Tribulations distinguish who is nearest and who is farthest.  Even in this world the value and position of those who are loving and loyal to the worldly rulers increases when they are steadfast in trials.  So you are not to sever your ties with the Truth at any cost.  Hold fast to the hem of truth and hold onto it with great strength and might.  If you are to form a bond with the Truth God then you will have to become true and you will have to compete with the difficulties arising in the path of being truthful only then will you observe how a new strength is born within you.


The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/pdf/Khutbat-e-Tahir-Vol-14-1995.pdf#page=489
